campbell.title: Campbell City Council — February 3, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to approve a $4,000 donation from Water Tower Kitchen to help pay for youth recreation scholarships. They also discussed extending a temporary local rule and moving forward with plans to build a bicycle bridge over Highway 17 at Hamilton Avenue, including applying for grant money to help pay for it.
keyTopics: $4,000 donation from Water Tower Kitchen for youth scholarships
Extension of temporary ordinance #2326
Bicycle bridge project over Highway 17 at Hamilton Avenue
Grant application for pedestrian and bike path improvements

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The city council held a public hearing on March 18, 2026 to get community input on a proposed new rule or policy. After hearing from residents, the council moved forward with introducing the ordinance and skipped the usual first formal reading to speed up the process. The measure is scheduled to come back for final approval at the next council meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing held to gather resident feedback on proposed ordinance
Council waived first reading to expedite the approval process
Ordinance scheduled for final adoption at next regular meeting
Community input was heard before moving forward with the measure

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 7, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on April 7, 2026, to approve meeting minutes from several sessions held in March and to authorize the Town Manager to sign a construction contract with QLM, Inc. The agenda was primarily administrative, focusing on paperwork approval and formal authorization for a construction project.
keyTopics: Approval of March meeting minutes (multiple sessions)
Construction contract authorization with QLM, Inc.
Town Manager given signing authority for the contract

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 21, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council met to approve several upgrades and agreements for city services. The main items included approving a new contract update for the airport's baggage handling system, protecting affordable housing if rental assistance programs end, updating the storm sewer system master plan, and moving forward with a family camp project funded by FEMA.
keyTopics: Airport baggage system upgrade contract
Affordable housing protections for low-income residents
Storm sewer system planning and improvements
FEMA-funded family camp project

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 14, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council met to review the city's spending plans for the next fiscal year, discussing which capital projects (like infrastructure and facilities) would be funded. The council also officially recognized Teen Month and National Volunteer Week to celebrate young people and community volunteers in Mountain View.
keyTopics: Next year's capital improvement budget and which city projects will be funded
Teen Month recognition and celebration
National Volunteer Week proclamation

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 7, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale City Council held a meeting on April 7, 2026, where they opened with a flag salute and highlighted two important observances: Arab-American Heritage Month and Fair Housing Month. These special recognitions aimed to celebrate the city's diverse communities and promote equal housing opportunities for all residents.
keyTopics: Arab-American Heritage Month recognition
Fair Housing Month observance
Community diversity celebration
Housing equality initiatives

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — April 13, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council held a community workshop to teach residents how to use OpenGov, a tool that shows where the city spends its money and how budgets work. The council also reminded people about rules requiring anyone who tries to influence city decisions to register as a lobbyist and report their activities.
keyTopics: OpenGov financial transparency tool demo and public access
City budget review and resident engagement
Lobbyist registration requirements under Cupertino Municipal Code Chapter 2.100
Reporting requirements for those influencing city decisions

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 16, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The City Council meeting on April 16, 2026 included important legal notices for Santa Clara residents. If you disagree with a city decision and want to take legal action, you have 90 days from when the decision is final to file a lawsuit, unless state law allows a shorter timeframe.
keyTopics: 90-day deadline to challenge city decisions in court
Legal disclosure requirements for quasi-adjudicative decisions
California Code of Civil Procedure Section 1094.6 rules

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 7, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met on April 7, 2026 to handle several administrative items including updates to the city's federal funding plan for 2025-2030 and review of council members' requested projects for the upcoming year. The council also discussed changes to the process for filling vacant council seats and approved meeting minutes and calendars.
keyTopics: Updates to the city's federal funding plan (Consolidated Plan) for 2025-2030
Review of new project requests submitted by individual council members
Changes to how council vacancy seats are filled
Approval of meeting minutes and upcoming council calendar

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — April 9, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council held a public hearing about a building at 405 Kipling Street in downtown. The owner wants to officially recognize it as a historically important building and get permission to add 5,000 square feet of new space as a reward for preserving and upgrading the structure to meet earthquake safety standards.
keyTopics: 405 Kipling Street historic building upgrade and expansion request
5,000 square feet of bonus floor area for seismic safety improvements
Elevating building status from Category 4 to Category 2 historic resource
Downtown Commercial zone development in Palo Alto
Historic Resources Board meeting schedule and minutes approval
